plan.thesis: Prompt caching lets you pay once to store a large context and reuse it at a fraction of the cost — but only when applied to the right workloads.
plan.targetTakeaway: Readers will leave knowing exactly which of their LLM calls are cacheable, how to configure caching in the Anthropic API, and what 60–90% cost reductions look like in practice.

sections.title: Why Your LLM Costs Are Higher Than They Need to Be
sections.intent: Hook the reader with a concrete cost-pain scenario; introduce caching as the solution.
keyPoints: A typical RAG call re-sends the same 10k-token system prompt every time
That's ~$0.03 per call multiplied by millions of requests
Prompt caching changes the economics fundamentally

sections.title: How Prompt Caching Works: Tokens, TTL, and Cache Keys
sections.intent: Explain the technical mechanism: what gets cached, for how long, and how the provider identifies cache hits.
keyPoints: Cache is keyed on the exact prefix of the messages array
Anthropic's TTL is 5 minutes (extendable via usage)
Input tokens on a cache hit cost ~10% of full price
cache_control breakpoint markers in the API

sections.title: When Caching Pays Off (and When It Doesn't)
sections.intent: Give engineers a decision framework: which call patterns benefit most and which don't.
keyPoints: High-frequency calls with a stable large prefix are ideal
One-off calls or rapidly changing contexts see little benefit
Multi-turn chat with a long system prompt is a perfect candidate
Short contexts where prefix is <1k tokens are rarely worth caching

sections.title: Step-by-Step: Adding Caching to a Claude API Call
sections.intent: Walk through a concrete code example showing the before/after diff and the resulting cost calculation.
keyPoints: Original call: 12k tokens, $0.036 each
After cache_control marker: first call $0.036, subsequent hits $0.004
Code snippet in Python using the official SDK
Measuring cache hit rate via usage.cache_read_input_tokens

sections.title: Anthropic vs. OpenAI: How the Implementations Differ
sections.intent: Brief comparison so readers can apply the concept if they use multiple providers.
keyPoints: OpenAI uses automatic prompt caching with no explicit markers
Anthropic requires explicit cache_control breakpoints
TTL and pricing tiers differ — show a side-by-side table

sections.title: Summary and Checklist
sections.intent: Recap key points as an actionable checklist readers can bookmark.
keyPoints: Identify stable large prefixes in your existing LLM calls
Add cache_control markers and instrument cache hit rate
Re-evaluate TTL strategy for long sessions

profile.name: Habr
profile.audience: Russian-speaking backend engineers with 3–10 years of experience

hypotheses.text: Re-sending a large system prompt on every LLM call incurs measurable and significant per-request costs
Cache hit tokens cost approximately 10% of full input token price on Anthropic
Anthropic's 5-minute TTL window provides sufficient cache reuse for typical API usage patterns
Multi-turn chat with a stable large system prompt benefits most from prompt caching
Adding cache_control markers to a real API call reduces costs by 60–90% for high-frequency workloads
OpenAI automatic caching and Anthropic explicit markers yield different cost profiles for the same workload
